data.title: Dave announces up to $50M class A stock buyback program
data.description:
• Neobank and fintech company Dave (NASDAQ:DAVE) announced on Monday an up to $50M class A share repurchase program.
  • DAVE shares were trading -2.77% pre-market at $81.05.
  • The company said the repurchase program does not have a fixed expiration date and may be executed from time to time based on market conditions, liquidity, and other strategic considerations.
  • The purchases may be made through a variety of methods, including open market transactions and privately negotiated transactions.
  • Dave expects to begin the buybacks imminently.

data.title: Flux Power appoints Krishna Vanka as CEO
data.description:
• Flux Power (NASDAQ:FLUX) said on Monday that it has appointed Krishna Vanka as Chief Executive Officer.
  • Vanka assumes the position on March 10, 2025, to replace Ron Dutt, whose planned retirement was previously announced.
  • Vanka was most recently the CEO of Fluence Digital, a part of Fluence Energy, a Nasdaq listed global market leader in energy storage.
  • Flux Power (FLUX) shares were up more than 4% in pre-market trading.

data.title: Braemar Hotels & Resorts refinances five hotels and reduces interest cots
data.description:
• Braemar Hotels & Resorts (NYSE:BHR) said on Monday that it has closed on a refinancing involving five hotels. The new loan totals $363M and has a two-year initial term with three one-year extension options, taking the final maturity to 2030.
  • The loan is interest only and provides for a floating interest rate of SOFR + 2.52%. The loan is secured by five hotels: The Clancy, The Notary Hotel, Marriott Seattle Waterfront, Sofitel Chicago Magnificent Mile, and The Ritz-Carlton Reserve Dorado Beach.
  • The new loan refinanced the $293.2M loan secured by The Clancy, The Notary Hotel, Marriott Seattle Waterfront, and Sofitel Chicago Magnificent Mile which had an interest rate of SOFR + 2.66% and had a final maturity date in June 2025 and the $62M secured by The Ritz-Carlton Reserve Dorado Beach which had an interest rate of SOFR + 4.75% and had a final maturity date in March 2026.
  • Shares of Braemar Hotels & Resorts (BHR) were down more than 12% in pre-market trading.

data.title: The Now Corporation announces plan to reduce debt
data.description:
• The Now Corporation (OTCPK:NWPN) said on Monday that it will reduce corporate debt to improve financial stability and boost shareholder value.
  • It will implementing measures to streamline operations, enhance balance sheet performance, and reduce financial liabilities.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Amentum announces launch of secondary offering
data.description:
• Amentum Holdings (NYSE:AMTM) on Monday announced the launch of a secondary underwritten public offering of 19.46M shares of its common stock.
  • Amentum is not selling any shares of common stock and will not receive any proceeds from the sale of the shares in the offering or from the debt-for-equity exchange.
  • In connection with the offering, a subsidiary of Jacobs Solutions is expected to exchange 19.46M shares of Amentum’s common stock for indebtedness of such subsidiary held by an affiliate of BofA Securities
  • Following the debt-for-equity exchange, if consummated, the selling shareholder intends to sell the shares to the underwriters in the offering. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Rio Tinto should pursue unification of dual-listed structure, Palliser says
data.description:
Activist investor Palliser Capital released a letter Monday to Rio Tinto's (NYSE:RIO) board urging further steps toward unifying its dual-listed structure after an appraisal report it commissioned supported its view that the miner should abandon its primary London listing and unify its corporate structure into a single Australian-domiciled company.

Palliser said the report by Grant Thornton highlighted a share price disparity between Rio's (NYSE:RIO) U.K. and Australian listings that limits company's ability to execute major stock-based mergers and acquisitions and complicates equity capital raising, supporting the hedge fund's contention that Rio was wrong to reject its calls to unify and urge its shareholders to vote against the resolution.

Rio (RIO) has said a review it conducted last year found that its dual-listed company structure continues to be effective and provides benefits to the company and shareholders.

The report's conclusions were published ahead of a vote by Rio (RIO) shareholders on Palliser's resolution calling for an independent review into its listing status; Rio's U.K.-listed shareholders will vote on April 3, while its Australian-listed shareholders will vote on May 1.

Rival BHP eliminated its dual-listing structure in 2022 and now primarily lists in Australia after facing pressure from activist investors.

data.title: Service Properties Trust appoints Chris Bilotto as CEO
data.description:
• Service Properties Trust (NASDAQ:SVC) said on Monday Chris Bilotto has been appointed as a managing trustee, president and chief executive officer of SVC, effective March 10, 2025.
  • Bilotto is an executive vice president of The RMR Group (RMR), overseeing its acquisition platform, asset management for all hotel and senior living properties, as well as property development and redevelopment throughout the United States. Prior to joining RMR, Bilotto worked at General Growth Properties in various management roles.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Adobe in spotlight as Citi sees 'mixed' Q1 amid recent software weakness
data.description:
Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE) was in the spotlight on Monday as Citi said it is “slightly cautious” on the Photoshop maker headed into its first-quarter results, amid recent weakness from some enterprise software companies.

Shares fell 1.3% in premarket trading.

“We are slightly cautious into Adobe’s FQ1 print given the softer outlook from CRM + other front-office names, though its valuation is less demanding vs. large-cap peers,” analyst Tyler Radke wrote in a note to clients. “Pricing may remain an important component in ADBE’s growth [algorithm] this year amid recent price updates to flagship apps and as weaker [Creative Cloud] web traffic could suggest paid user acquisition remains challenging.”

Radke kept his Neutral rating on the stock but lowered his price target to $490.

In recent months, Adobe upped the subscription price for 20GB of Photoshop by $5 to $14.99 a month, while it bumped Lightroom by $2 to $11.99 per month, effective January 15. Substance 3D will be raised by $10 per month to $59.99 per month, while the pre-paid plan goes to $599.98, effective March 25. However, Adobe also released a free Photoshop app for the iPhone last month.

When taken together, it suggests that Adobe can keep raising the prices on its core creative professional base in an effort to offset slower monetization down-market, “likely due to fiercer competition vs. Canva and other casual photo/video-editing freemium apps,” Radke suggested.

A consensus of analysts expect Adobe to earn $4.97 per share on $5.66B in revenue for the coming quarter.

data.title: NV5 announces $18 million in recent contract awards
data.description:
• NV5 Global (NASDAQ:NVEE) on Monday said it has been awarded $18 million in recent data center design and commissioning contracts throughout the U.S. and Asia.
  • NV5 will implement high-efficiency cooling and power distribution testing, inspection, certification and design solutions for both new and retrofitted facilities.
  • NVEE -0.71% premarket to $18.1.
  • Source: Press Release
